title: Cryptantha insolita
taxonomy: Cryptantha insolita is classified as a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It belongs to the genus Cryptantha and is a native plant of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification further categorizes it as a member of the tribe Cynoglosseae, within the family Boraginaceae. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as its leaf shape and arrangement, as well as its flower structure. Cryptantha insolita is often found growing in dry, sandy areas with full sun.

Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects.
